qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in public file-serving controllers exposes out-of-root and private files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is authenticated autocomplete SQL injection in `Core/Controller/CopyModel.php` and `Core/Model/CodeModel.php`, while this finding is unauthenticated filesystem path traversal in `Core/Controller/Files.php` and `Core/Controller/Myfiles.php`. It does not reach the advisory's SQL-construction root cause or equivalent source-to-sink path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory-described root cause. The advisory targets authenticated autocomplete SQL injection through attacker-controlled `source`, `fieldcode`, and `fieldtitle` reaching `CodeModel::all()`, but the only qualifying session finding is a separate public file-serving path traversal issue.
evidence: The advisory summary and patch `5c070f82665b98efd2f914a4769c6dc9415f5b0f` identify the vulnerable path as autocomplete requests reaching `Core/Controller/CopyModel.php` and unsafely concatenated SQL identifiers in `Core/Model/CodeModel.php`.

Local source confirms `CopyModel::privateCore()` handles authenticated `action=autocomplete` requests and `CodeModel::all()` builds SQL by concatenating `$fieldCode`, `$fieldDescription`, and `$tableName` directly into the query string.

The only qualifying finding in `.judge/input_manifest.json` is `file_serving_path_traversal`, which documents canonicalization and token-bypass flaws in `Core/Controller/Files.php` and `Core/Controller/Myfiles.php` that cause unauthorized file reads, not SQL injection in the autocomplete model lookup path.
